2605.02402.title: Automatic Reflection Level Classification in Hungarian Student Essays
2605.02402.paper_summary: Reflective thinking is a key competency in education, but assessing reflective writing remains a time-consuming and subjective task for education experts. While automated reflective analysis has been explored in several languages, Hungarian language was not researched extensively. In this paper, we present the first comprehensive study on automatic reflection level classification in Hungarian student essays. We used a large, expert-annotated Hungarian dataset consisting of 1,954 reflective essays collected over multiple academic years and labeled on a four-level reflection scale. We investigate two approaches: (1) classical machine learning models using TF-IDF and semantic embedding features, and (2) Hungarian-specific transformer models fine-tuned for document-level reflection classification. To address the strong class imbalance in the dataset, we systematically examine class weighting, oversampling, data augmentation, and alternative loss functions. An extensive ablation study is conducted to analyze the contribution of each modeling and balancing strategy. Our results show that shallow machine learning models with appropriate feature engineering achieve strong overall performance, reaching up to 71% overall score averaged over accuracy, F1-score, and ROC AUC metrics, while transformer-based models achieve slightly lower overall score (68%) averaged over the same metrics, but demonstrate better generalization on minority reflection classes. These findings highlight the continued relevance of classical methods for low-resource settings and the robustness of transformer models for imbalanced classification. The proposed dataset and experimental insights provide a solid foundation for future research on automated reflective analysis in Hungarian and other morphologically rich languages.
2605.02402.paper_summary_zh: 反思性思維是教育中的一項關鍵能力，但評估反思性寫作對教育專家來說仍然是一項耗時且主觀的任務。雖然自動反思分析在多種語言中已被探討，但匈牙利語的研究尚不深入。在本文中，我們展示了對匈牙利學生論文中自動反思水平分類的首次全面研究。我們使用了一個大型的專家標註匈牙利數據集，該數據集由1,954篇反思性論文組成，這些論文在多個學術年度內收集，並根據四級反思量表進行標註。我們研究了兩種方法：（1）使用TF-IDF和語義嵌入特徵的經典機器學習模型，以及（2）專門針對文檔級反思分類微調的匈牙利特定Transformer模型。為了解決數據集中強烈的類別不平衡問題，我們系統地檢查了類別加權、過採樣、數據增強和替代損失函數。進行了廣泛的消融研究，以分析每種建模和平衡策略的貢獻。我們的結果顯示，適當特徵工程的淺層機器學習模型在整體性能上表現出色，整體得分達到71%，該得分是基於準確率、F1分數和ROC AUC指標的平均值，而基於Transformer的模型在同樣指標的平均得分稍低（68%），但在少數反思類別上顯示出更好的泛化能力。這些發現突顯了經典方法在低資源環境中的持續相關性以及Transformer模型在不平衡分類中的穩健性。所提出的數據集和實驗見解為未來在匈牙利語及其他形態豐富語言中的自動反思分析研究提供了堅實的基礎。
2605.02402.author: Zsolt Csibi et.al.
2605.02402.authors: Zsolt Csibi, Mónika Sándor, Mónika Serfőző, Kinga Gyöngy, Kristian Fenech
